---
title: "Code"
---

I am passionate about writing robust, well-documented, and reproducible scientific code. 
My public repositories are hosted on [my GitHub page](https://github.com/your-username).

I believe research codes should be **open-source**, **reproducible**, and 
**freely available** to the scientific community.


## Solvers & Libraries

::: {.project-card}
### [ProjectName](https://github.com/your-username/project1) 🌊

**Description of your main code project.** For example: A high-order finite volume solver 
for incompressible Navier-Stokes equations, implemented in Julia. Features adaptive mesh 
refinement, parallel computation via MPI, and post-processing tools.

**Language:** Julia | **License:** MIT  
[[Documentation]](https://your-username.github.io/project1/) 
[[GitHub]](https://github.com/your-username/project1)
:::

::: {.project-card}
### [AnotherProject](https://github.com/your-username/project2) 🔧

**MATLAB toolbox for [specific task].** For example: A collection of MATLAB functions for 
spectral analysis of turbulent flow data, including energy spectra computation, 
structure functions, and proper orthogonal decomposition (POD).

**Language:** MATLAB | **License:** GPL-3.0  
[[GitHub]](https://github.com/your-username/project2)
:::

::: {.project-card}
### [UtilityScripts](https://github.com/your-username/project3) ⚙️

**Miscellaneous utilities.** Post-processing scripts, mesh generation tools, 
and visualization helpers used across my research projects.

**Languages:** Julia, MATLAB, Python  
[[GitHub]](https://github.com/your-username/project3)
:::


## Notebooks & Tutorials

These are interactive computational notebooks showcasing specific analyses and methods. 
Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) — code and results side by side.

| Topic | Language | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| [Lid-driven cavity benchmark](code/lid-driven-cavity.qmd) | Julia | Validation of my solver against benchmark data |
| [Spectral analysis of turbulence](code/spectral-analysis.qmd) | MATLAB | Computing energy spectra from DNS data |
| [Mesh convergence study](code/mesh-convergence.qmd) | Julia | Grid independence analysis for channel flow |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Reproducibility
All notebooks include the complete code to reproduce the results. 
Clone the [repository](https://github.com/your-username/your-site) 
and run them locally with Quarto.
:::
